heading: Fuel cycle research, development, demonstration, and commercial application
label: 16273 Fuel cycle research, development, demonstration, and commercial application
notes: Amendments2020— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section related to advanced fuel cycle initiative.Pub. L. 116–2602018—Subsec. (a). struck out “, acting through the Director of the Office of Nuclear Energy, Science and Technology,” after “The Secretary”.Pub. L. 115–248
amendments: Amendments2020— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section related to advanced fuel cycle initiative.Pub. L. 116–2602018—Subsec. (a). struck out “, acting through the Director of the Office of Nuclear Energy, Science and Technology,” after “The Secretary”.Pub. L. 115–248

children.heading: Used nuclear fuel research, development, demonstration, and commercial application
children.label: a Used nuclear fuel research, development, demonstration, and commercial application
A
children.text: dry cask storage;

children.label: B
children.text: consolidated interim storage;

children.label: C
children.text: deep geological storage and disposal, including mined repository, and other technologies;

children.label: D
children.text: used nuclear fuel transportation;

children.label: E
children.text: integrated waste management systems;

children.label: F
children.text: vitrification;

children.label: G
children.text: fuel recycling and transmutation technologies, including advanced reprocessing technologies such as electrochemical and molten salt technologies, and advanced redox extraction technologies;

children.label: H
children.text: advanced materials to be used in subparagraphs (A) through (G); and

children.label: I
children.text: other areas as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: ensure all activities and designs incorporate state of the art safeguards technologies and techniques to reduce risk of proliferation;

children.label: B
children.text: consult with the Administrator of the National Nuclear Security Administration to integrate safeguards and security by design;

children.label: C
children.text: consider the potential benefits and other impacts of those activities for civilian nuclear applications, environmental health and safety, and national security, including consideration of public consent; and

children.label: D
children.text: consider the economic viability of all activities and designs.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 3 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out the program under this subsection $60,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2025.

children.label: A
children.text: performance;

children.label: B
children.text: accident tolerance;

children.label: C
children.text: proliferation resistance;

children.label: D
children.text: use of resources;

children.label: E
children.text: environmental impact; and

children.label: F
children.text: economics.

children.heading: Requirements
children.label: 2 Requirements
children.text: In carrying out the program under this subsection, the Secretary shall focus on the development of advanced technology fuels, including fabrication techniques, that offer improved accident-tolerance and economic performance with the goal of initial commercial application by .December 31, 2025

children.heading: Report
children.label: 3 Report
children.text: Not later than 180 days , the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate a report that describes how the technologies and concepts studied under this program would impact reactor economics, the fuel cycle, operations, safety, proliferation, and the environment.December 27, 2020

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out the program under this subsection $125,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2025.
